bonuses.leadin: This character is able to join the Army of the North by shooting a hole through a silver coin that was tossed into the air. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who seeks a glorious death only to die by being shot in the back by Tomas Arroyo. This character's corpse is then buried in Arlington National Cemetery by Harriet Winslow.
This author of The Death of Artemio Cruz fictionalized the death of Ambrose Bierce in his novel The Old Gringo.

Fuentes also wrote a novel titled [this first name] Unborn in which the couple Angel and Angeles attempt to have a child on October 12, 1992, exactly five hundred years after an explorer with this first name "discovered" the New World.
answers_sanitized: the old gringo [or gringo viejo; accept Ambrose Bierce]
Christopher [accept Christopher Columbus; accept Christopher Unborn or Cristobal Nonato]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This character is able to join the Army of the North by shooting a hole through a silver coin that was tossed into the air. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this character who seeks a glorious death only to die by being shot in the back by Tomas Arroyo. This character's corpse is then buried in Arlington National Cemetery by Harriet Winslow.
This author of The Death of Artemio Cruz fictionalized the death of Ambrose Bierce in his novel The Old Gringo.

Fuentes also wrote a novel titled [this first name] Unborn in which the couple Angel and Angeles attempt to have a child on October 12, 1992, exactly five hundred years after an explorer with this first name "discovered" the New World.

bonuses.leadin: This last of Fuentes’s major novels borrows its structure from Tristram Shandy. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Angel and Angeles scheme to have a child born at exactly midnight of October 12, 1992, in order to win a government prize for the five hundred year anniversary of the discovery of the Americas.
In a Fuentes story, this type of reclining statue popular among several Mesoamerican peoples turns into flesh, becomes animate, and kills the man who purchased it.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This last of Fuentes's major novels borrows its structure from Tristram Shandy. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel in which Angel and Angeles scheme to have a child born at exactly midnight of October 12, 1992, in order to win a government prize for the five hundred year anniversary of the discovery of the Americas.
In a Fuentes story, this type of reclining statue popular among several Mesoamerican peoples turns into flesh, becomes animate, and kills the man who purchased it.

bonuses.leadin: A character created by this author sells sausages out of his suitcase to finance his trip from New York to Argus, North Dakota. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this half-German-American, half-Chippewa author of The Master Builder’s Singing Club, who described a love triangle between Mary Lazarre, Lulu Lamartine, and Nector Kashpaw in her episodic debut novel, Love Medicine.

Like much Native American fiction, Erdrich’s Love Medicine is set in one of these areas allotted to the Chippewa people. Sherman Alexie wrote about Spokane Indians who live in one of these places, in a novel titled for their Blues.

Erdrich and her husband Michael Dorris co-wrote a novel in which two academics try to recover this historical figure’s crown. In a 1987 novel, a young couple scheme to win a government prize by conceiving a son exactly on the anniversary of one of this man’s accomplishments.
answers: Christopher Columbus [or Cristoforo Colombo; those books are The Crown of Columbus and Christopher Unborn]
answers_sanitized: Christopher Columbus [or Cristoforo Colombo; those books are The Crown of Columbus and Christopher Unborn]
